message:
So last spring we got that surprise ice storm, and I realized I'd never actually *looked* at our flashlights since... I dunno, 2019? Found three with dead batteries, one that only flickered like a creepy haunted-house prop, and one I'm pretty sure belonged to a former tenant.

I stood there in my kitchen at 6 PM laughing at the absurdity—*of course* the one moment I needed light was the moment I'd done zero maintenance. My kids thought I'd lost it.

Turns out, being ready doesn't mean being paranoid. It just means spending like 20 minutes every couple seasons checking the basics: batteries work, supplies haven't expired, routes are clear. No drama, no doomsday energy.

Now I actually *enjoy* knowing we're set. Way less funny than dead flashlights, but way more peaceful.

What's your 'I should've checked that sooner' storm story? 😄
